title: Supporters not ruling out new legal fight if push to remove Columbus statue continues
summary: The Columbus Monument Corporation is signaling willingness to pursue additional legal action if Syracuse's incoming administration continues efforts to remove and relocate the Christopher Columbus statue to a proposed Heritage Park. Current Mayor Walsh stated the statue will remain through his term; Deputy Mayor Sharon Owens (mayoral candidate) indicated Heritage Park would be a 2026 priority, while other candidates oppose removal. Monument Corp. member Nick Pirro indicated courts ruled their previous lawsuit premature but legal options remain available.

political_signal.description: Monument Corporation explicitly reserving legal remedies and framing removal as unresolved despite prior court ruling; mayoral transition creates uncertainty as incoming administration may reprioritize or deprioritize Heritage Park initiative.
